analysis: The index-tuning search found a configuration that improved the total p50 time by 94.1% compared to the baseline with no indexes. The best configuration included multiple indexes on different columns of the orders, part, customer, lineitem, and nation tables, suggesting that the search was able to identify a set of indexes that effectively sped up the queries. However, the search also found that some queries, specifically q6, q7, q11, q13, q18, and q22, regressed compared to the baseline, which likely means that the added indexes introduced additional overhead for these queries. The storage used by the best configuration was 22.7 MB, which may be a reasonable trade-off for the significant improvement in performance. The result looks credible given the substantial improvement in p50 time, but further investigation is needed to understand the regressions and determine whether the added indexes are worth the potential overhead. Overall, the search appears to have found a configuration that significantly improved performance, but may require additional tuning to mitigate the regressions.
